architecture.summary: DISC is a constrained decoding algorithm that modifies autoregressive LLM generation by dynamically adjusting sampling probabilities to satisfy set-based constraints while reducing distributional bias. During generation, it iteratively samples candidate sequences using a modified token distribution, verifies constraint compliance through Parallel Prefix-Verification (PPV), and accepts/rejects samples probabilistically. PPV replaces CPU-based prefix trees with GPU-optimized binary search over a sorted constraint array, eliminating device communication bottlenecks. Information flows from the LLM's token logits → top-k selection → PPV binary search verification → acceptance/rejection with importance-weighted probabilities → final constrained output.

biblio.title: Efficient and Asymptotically Unbiased Constrained Decoding for Large Language Models

claims.statement: For arbitrary probability value ε, there exists an autoregressive model and a candidate set with P_θ(S̄) ≥ ε, such that the KL divergence between constrained distribution and true constrained distribution is lower bounded by Ω(ε log(1/ε)).
DISC achieves asymptotic unbiasedness with KL divergence bounded by O((P_θ(S̄) / T_max) log(1/ε)), exponentially fast convergence as P_θ(S̄) → 0, fixed expected sampling size.

claims.dataset: Entity Disambiguation (6 datasets: ACE2004, AIDA, AQUAINT, CWEB, MSNBC, WIKI)
claims.statement: On Entity Disambiguation with T_max=6, average Micro F1 gain compared to Vanilla increased from +4.50% (trie-based) to +5.00% (DISC) for the BLINK/AY2 model.

claims.dataset: Document Retrieval (11 datasets: AY2, CWEB, ELI5, FEV, HoPo, NQ, zsRE, TREx, TQA, WNED, WoW)
claims.statement: On Document Retrieval with T_max=6, DISC achieves average R-Precision gain of +8.75% compared to Vanilla, nearly double the improvement achieved by trie-based method (+4.76%).

On Entity Retrieval (DBPedia-Entity) with T_max=4, DISC achieves relevance score of 0.735, more than doubling Vanilla (0.340) and exceeding trie-based (0.600), realized with inference time of 76.88s versus trie-based 171.73s.
On Fact Checking (Climate-FEVER) with T_max=3, DISC achieves relevance score of 0.290, marking +84.44% improvement over Vanilla (0.160), with inference time of 149.9s compared to trie-based 234.8s.

Parallel Prefix-Verification (PPV) eliminates need for CPU-based prefix trees, removes frequent data transfers between devices, and is parallelizable across batches and top tokens with O(log|S̄|) binary search time complexity per candidate verification.

DISC can be seamlessly integrated with existing generation strategies (temperature scaling, top-k sampling) and task-specific techniques (special tokenizations, retrieval tricks) without requiring modifications to the underlying LLM architecture.
